organizations.name: Crown Bioscience
organizations.address: 16550 West Bernardo Drive, Building 5, Suite 525, San Diego, CA 92127, USA
organizations.description: Crown Bioscience is a CRO specializing in translational oncology and immuno-oncology drug discovery and development. Its preclinical and translational services help biotech and pharmaceutical companies make faster, more predictive R&D decisions.

organizations.name: The Douglas Brain Bank
organizations.description: The Douglas Brain Bank, based at the Douglas Mental Health University Institute and affiliated with McGill University, is a leading repository of human brain tissue dedicated to advancing neuroscience research. Established in 1980, it houses thousands of well-preserved brains from donors with psychiatric, neurodegenerative, and other neurological conditions, alongside healthy controls. Each specimen is linked to rich clinical, demographic, and developmental data, enabling researchers worldwide to study brain disorders and drive discoveries in mental health and neurodegeneration. By maintaining rigorous preservation and annotation standards, the Douglas Brain Bank ensures high-quality resources for translational and basic science research.

organizations.name: The Windber Biobank
organizations.description: The Windber Biobank, part of the Chan Soon-Shiong Institute of Molecular Medicine at Windber (formerly Windber Research Institute), is a CAP-accredited and CLIA-certified facility established in 2001 to support translational cancer research. It houses hundreds of thousands of high-quality, well-annotated human biospecimens—including blood, plasma, and solid tissues—and provides advanced processing, storage, and data-tracking services. The biobank supports major Department of Defense and NIH programs, serving as a centralized resource for precision medicine and proteogenomic studies.

organizations.name: Northwestern Nervous System Tumor Bank
organizations.address: 303 E. Superior St., Simpson Querrey Building 6-500, Chicago, IL 60611
organizations.description: The Northwestern University Nervous System Tumor Bank (NSTB) provides scientists with high-quality, patient-derived biospecimens (tissues, cells and blood) to support preclinical and translational neuro-oncology research. We offer a comprehensive platform for biospecimen and data banking, including protocol and informed consent tracking, sample annotation, inventory management and integration with sample processing and analysis technologies. The NSTB is funded in part through the Robert H. Lurie Comprehensive Cancer Center of Northwestern University's NCI P50 SPORE grant in Brain Tumor Research, as well as through the generous support of the Lou and Jean Malnati Brain Tumor Institute of the Lurie Cancer Center.

organizations.name: AminoChain
organizations.description: Accelerating research and empowering patients by simplifying biospecimen discovery and acquisition.

organizations.name: ImYoo
organizations.description: ImYoo conducts patient-centric immune discovery studies by enabling patients to self-collect small volumes of blood at home for high-resolution single-cell genomics assays.

organizations.name: Shlok Superspecialty Care
organizations.description: In collaboration with various institutions, hospitals, and collection centers, we recruit patients specifically for participation in clinical research and biospecimen studies.

organizations.description: We provide human biospecimens worldwide to advance biomedical/bioscience research & development.

organizations.name: Ino Specimen Biobank
organizations.description: INOVIE Group, major French network of clinical laboratories gives you access to characterized patients specimens. Its dedicated company, INO Specimens Biobank, ISB, interfaces contacts with 700 clinical labs, 27 Central Laboratories, all together testing 90 000 patients files per day, on a test menu with more than 600 different markers (+120 autoimmune markers, +400 allergens for specific IgE quantification). More than 20 000 specimens are immediately available.

organizations.name: Anagram Research Solutions
organizations.description: Anagram Research Solutions is a biobank and research organization that provides high-quality biospecimens for medical research.

organizations.name: Ukraine Research Group
organizations.description: The mission of the Ukraine Research Group is to promote the development of advanced technologies for precision biomedical research. We focus on the development of biobanks and the use of high-quality biospecimens for medical research.

organizations.description: Ukraine Association of Biobank is the premier biorepository of human tissue samples in the industry.

organizations.description: ProteoGenex is an established innovative provider of human biological specimens and clinical information of the highest quality.

organizations.name: BIOMEDICA CRO
organizations.description: We do biosample procurement through a broad network of partnered hospitals in Ukraine and supply them globally. Human biospecimen collection is an easy task with us.

organizations.name: The Neuro C-BIG Repository
organizations.address: 3801 University Street Montreal, QC H3A 2B4 Canada
organizations.description: A growing collection of biospecimens, longitudinal clinical and neuropsychiatric information, imaging and genetic data from patients with neurological disease as well as healthy controls.

organizations.name: Stibion
organizations.description: Stichting Biobank Oost Nederland (Stibion), based in the Netherlands, manages and distributes urine and plasma samples from an active cohort of individuals aged 45 and older. The study collects urine and health data every 4 months, and blood samples once a year, offering a rich resource for population health and biomarker research.

organizations.name: Precision Ocular Network
organizations.description: The Precision Ocular Biobank, a division of Precision Ocular Network, is one of the world’s only certified Ocular Biobanks. Featuring human post-mortem, normal and diseased fixed and frozen whole eyes, aqueous, vitreous, cells and blood. Diseases include AMD, Glaucoma, Diabetic Retinopathy and more.

organizations.name: CHTN Eastern
organizations.description: Serving the Research Community with Quality Biospecimens for more than 30 years!

organizations.name: France Tissue Bank
organizations.description:
France Tissue Bank – Your trusted partner for high-quality, responsive biospecimen sourcing

Looking for high-standard, reliable samples with fast turnaround times? France Tissue Bank (FTB) is your ideal partner.

🔹 Specialized in oncology and working closely with Anatomo-Cyto-Pathology laboratories, FTB provides premium human biospecimens including FFPE blocks, histological slides, liquid-based PAP smears, and digital pathology images.

We support cutting-edge companies operating in 
✔️ Artificial Intelligence, 
✔️ Digital Health, 
✔️ MedTech, 
✔️ Molecular and In Vitro Diagnostics, 
✔️ Drug Development, 
✔️ Biotechnology, 
✔️ Precision Medicine
✔️ Translational Research 
✔️ CRO

Our services include:
🔷Supply of biospecimens (FFPE blocks, slide sets, smears, digital images)
🔷Medical and pathological expertise
🔷Special staining and molecular biology testing
🔷Cession of multimodal datasets (images, annotations, clinical and molecular data)
🔷Customized services to support new therapeutic or technological developments

🎯 Our mission: to accelerate cancer research and innovation by providing high-quality, ethically sourced biological materials and data within a compliant and responsive framework.

organizations.name: CSD Bio
organizations.description: CSD BIO is Ukrainian Biobank prone to empower researchers with the tools for advancing precision medicine​. Our biorepository offers an extensive inventory of biospecimens as well as an exclusive laboratory and research services.
